name: Lennie Hayton
abstract: Leonard George Hayton (February 14, 1908 – April 24, 1971) was an American musician, composer, conductor and arranger. Hayton's trademark was a captain's hat, which he always wore at a rakish angle.

concerts.label: Frank Sinatra and Lena Horne, Singers
Frank Sinatra and Lena Horne, Singers

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: And through that eight years we played, I played for some of the most notable people of the times during that era. And I became — her husband, Lennie Hayton, he became my mentor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 30, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: So the next rehearsal all of a sudden man, here’s Lena Horne. We meet and this little guy turned out to be Lenny Hayton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: the only other man that I learned -- well, several people I've learned a lot from -- was Lenny Hayton

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I got Lennie Hayton, who was . . . I used to work for him, in the Ipana program.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren M. "Tex" Brewster
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer: 'Leighton? Lenny Hayton? No I can't tell you he married that uh was she a singer? ... Yeah that's the one he had that's Lenny Hayton? Yeah huh? That's Lenny Hayton? Lenny Hayton? Yeah well anyway uh and he was in with those guys'

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: when she married Lenny Hayton [phonetic] I started paying attention, because not only, you know, he taught her everything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
